sys_metadata_link.payload:
true text 89137fd0db4420106a58ef92ca961903 false false false false false 6a0e0e96db4d99501e1792c5d4961956 ddba8256db4d99501e1792c5d496195a The Tasker Application Administrator role enables an administrator to manage Tasker tables and assign KB0010002 2022-08-03 4.67 Configuring a Group for the Tasker Application Administrator Role kb_knowledge rjoy 2022-08-03 19:07:40 global / f37ecad6db4d99501e1792c5d496199b 12 csloyer 2022-09-30 14:02:55 341 The Tasker Application Administrator role enables an administrator to manage Tasker tables and assign top level permissions to all users.

Best Practice: Minimize the number of users assigned to this group.

  1. Select the ellipsis to the right of the role name. Select Add Group from the popup.

  1. In the Add Group popup, enter the Name and Description. Select Save from the context menu.

  1. Select the group from the drop-down.

 

  1. Add the role to the group. The easiest way to do this is to select Add the role now, as shown above.
  2. Add users to the group:
    1. Click the Group Members button.
    2. Select Open in Platform from the context menu. (See picture below.)
    3. Click New, select a user, and click Submit.
    4. Add additional users, as necessary.

If users:

  • Have not previously been assigned a Tasker role, they are assigned the Tasker role for the group. For example, adding a user to the Tasker Global Hierarchy Administrator role assigns the GHA role to the user.
  • Have been previously assigned a Tasker role on user records, rather than via a Tasker group, select “Let’s fix it” to right of Users explicitly assigned role.

After selecting groups for all Tasker roles, select the Uses Groups for Roles checkbox and click Save Changes. After creating and selecting groups, use the Counts tab to manage group assignments.

Counts Tab Overview

The Counts tab is available for each Tasker role and includes the following:

  • Group Members lists the users assigned to the Tasker group. Any messages relating to the group (such as “The group is missing the role”) appear above the list). You can add or remove users from the group as detailed in the Group Members Count article, under Related Articles.
  • Has Role Explicitly lists the users in your organization whose Tasker role was assigned to their user records.

Note: If your organization is upgrading from an earlier version of Tasker (2.7.x or earlier) or your organization chooses to enable Group Management after initially installing, configuring, and using Tasker, the Has Role Explicitly list will include all Tasker users. When there are users who have the Tasker role explicitly assigned, the “Let’s fix it” displays under the Tasker role and in the Has Role Explicitly list. Select “Let’s fix it” to add these users to the selected group. For more information, review the Has Role Explicitly Count article under Related Articles.

  • Role Members lists users who have been assigned a Tasker role in the ServiceNow Role Members table. The list displays users and their Tasker role. Users may appear more than once if they are assigned a role through group membership and inherited the role from assignment to another role. (For example, assigning a user to the Global Template Designer role, automatically assigns the Personal Template Designer role to the user.)  See the Role Members Count article, under Related Articles, for more information.
  • Member Table Roles lists users assigned the Tasker role in Tasker. (Users and roles are stored in the Tasker Member Table Roles table.) This number may differ from the ServiceNow Role Members count. When this occurs, a “Let’s fix it” message displays. Selecting “Let’s fix it”) synchronizes Role Members with Member Table Roles. However, this message does not necessarily indicate an issue. Refer to the Member Table Roles Count article, under Related Articles, for more information.
